attributes.description: Deep dive into connection capacity and resource contention. Are we approaching limits? How are sessions distributed?

query.esql:
TS metrics-oracledbreceiver.otel-*
| WHERE oracledb.processes.usage IS NOT NULL OR oracledb.processes.limit IS NOT NULL
| STATS usage = MAX(LAST_OVER_TIME(oracledb.processes.usage)), limit_val = MAX(LAST_OVER_TIME(oracledb.processes.limit)) BY time_bucket = BUCKET(@timestamp, 20, ?_tstart, ?_tend)
| SORT time_bucket ASC

TS metrics-oracledbreceiver.otel-*
| WHERE oracledb.processes.usage IS NOT NULL OR oracledb.processes.limit IS NOT NULL
| STATS usage = MAX(LAST_OVER_TIME(oracledb.processes.usage)), limit_val = MAX(LAST_OVER_TIME(oracledb.processes.limit)) BY time_bucket = BUCKET(@timestamp, 20, ?_tstart, ?_tend)
| SORT time_bucket ASC

attributes.title: Process Usage vs Limit Over Time

embeddableConfig.description: OS process count and configured PROCESSES limit plotted over time.

query.esql:
FROM metrics-oracledbreceiver.otel-*
| WHERE oracledb.sessions.usage IS NOT NULL OR oracledb.sessions.limit IS NOT NULL
| EVAL ts_sec = DATE_TRUNC(1 second, @timestamp)
| STATS usage_sec = SUM(oracledb.sessions.usage), limit_sec = MAX(oracledb.sessions.limit) BY ts_sec
| STATS usage = AVG(usage_sec), limit_val = MAX(limit_sec) BY time_bucket = BUCKET(ts_sec, 20, ?_tstart, ?_tend)
| SORT time_bucket ASC

FROM metrics-oracledbreceiver.otel-*
| WHERE oracledb.sessions.usage IS NOT NULL OR oracledb.sessions.limit IS NOT NULL
| EVAL ts_sec = DATE_TRUNC(1 second, @timestamp)
| STATS usage_sec = SUM(oracledb.sessions.usage), limit_sec = MAX(oracledb.sessions.limit) BY ts_sec
| STATS usage = AVG(usage_sec), limit_val = MAX(limit_sec) BY time_bucket = BUCKET(ts_sec, 20, ?_tstart, ?_tend)
| SORT time_bucket ASC

attributes.title: Session Usage vs Limit Over Time

embeddableConfig.description: Total session count and configured session limit plotted over time.

query.esql:
FROM metrics-oracledbreceiver.otel-*
| WHERE oracledb.sessions.usage IS NOT NULL
| EVAL ts_sec = DATE_TRUNC(1 second, @timestamp)
| STATS total_per_sec = SUM(oracledb.sessions.usage) BY ts_sec
| STATS total_sessions = AVG(total_per_sec) BY time_bucket = BUCKET(ts_sec, 20, ?_tstart, ?_tend)
| SORT time_bucket ASC

FROM metrics-oracledbreceiver.otel-*
| WHERE oracledb.sessions.usage IS NOT NULL

| STATS total_per_sec = SUM(oracledb.sessions.usage) BY ts_sec
| STATS total_sessions = AVG(total_per_sec) BY time_bucket = BUCKET(ts_sec, 20, ?_tstart, ?_tend)
| SORT time_bucket ASC

embeddableConfig.description: Aggregate session count over time, summing all session types and statuses.

query.esql:
FROM metrics-oracledbreceiver.otel-*
| WHERE oracledb.sessions.usage IS NOT NULL
| EVAL ts_sec = DATE_TRUNC(1 second, @timestamp)
| STATS session_total = SUM(oracledb.sessions.usage) BY ts_sec, session_type = attributes.session_type
| STATS session_count = AVG(session_total) BY time_bucket = BUCKET(ts_sec, 20, ?_tstart, ?_tend), session_type
| SORT time_bucket ASC

FROM metrics-oracledbreceiver.otel-*
| WHERE oracledb.sessions.usage IS NOT NULL
| EVAL ts_sec = DATE_TRUNC(1 second, @timestamp)
| STATS session_total = SUM(oracledb.sessions.usage) BY ts_sec, session_type = attributes.session_type
| STATS session_count = AVG(session_total) BY time_bucket = BUCKET(ts_sec, 20, ?_tstart, ?_tend), session_type
| SORT time_bucket ASC

embeddableConfig.description: Session count over time broken down by session_type attribute (USER vs BACKGROUND). Stacked area showing the composition of total sessions.

| EVAL limit_val = CASE(raw_limit > 0, raw_limit, NULL)

query.esql:
FROM metrics-oracledbreceiver.otel-*
| WHERE oracledb.sessions.usage IS NOT NULL OR oracledb.sessions.limit IS NOT NULL
| EVAL ts_sec = DATE_TRUNC(1 second, @timestamp)
| STATS total_usage = SUM(oracledb.sessions.usage), limit_val = MAX(oracledb.sessions.limit) BY ts_sec
| WHERE limit_val IS NOT NULL AND total_usage IS NOT NULL AND limit_val > 0
| SORT ts_sec DESC
| LIMIT 1
| EVAL utilisation_pct = ROUND(TO_DOUBLE(total_usage) / TO_DOUBLE(limit_val), 3)

FROM metrics-oracledbreceiver.otel-*
| WHERE oracledb.sessions.usage IS NOT NULL OR oracledb.sessions.limit IS NOT NULL
| EVAL ts_sec = DATE_TRUNC(1 second, @timestamp)
| STATS total_usage = SUM(oracledb.sessions.usage), limit_val = MAX(oracledb.sessions.limit) BY ts_sec
| WHERE limit_val IS NOT NULL AND total_usage IS NOT NULL AND limit_val > 0
| SORT ts_sec DESC
| LIMIT 1
| EVAL utilisation_pct = ROUND(TO_DOUBLE(total_usage) / TO_DOUBLE(limit_val), 3)

embeddableConfig.content:
# Oracle Database - Resource capacity, session & locking

This dashboard offers insights into resource capacity, session distribution, and lock contention for your Oracle Database instance, including

- Process and session utilisation against configured limits
- Active transaction count and transaction usage trends over time
- DML lock and enqueue lock utilisation
- Session distribution by type (user vs background) and status (active vs inactive)
- Process, session, transaction, and lock usage vs limit trends over time
- Enqueue resource usage over time

attributes.title: [Metrics Oracle] Resource Capacity, Sessions & Locking
